title: Acronia constricta
taxonomy: Acronia constricta is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Borneo and is classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree. The plant is part of the Acronia genus, which consists of several species found in Southeast Asia. Acronia constricta is closely related to other species within the Rubiaceae family, sharing similar characteristics and growth habits. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships within the Acronia genus.

intro: Acronia constricta, also known as the Philippine acronia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Philippines. This plant is prized for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, fever, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Acronia constricta include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
